messages.content: You must return JSON only. ALL fields in the schema must be present, even if null or empty.

Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend roughly a few times weekly on social media

response_parsed.opinion: As someone who's anxious and spends a fair amount of time on social media, I've come to realize that dealing with fake content is a constant battle. For me, it's about setting boundaries and being more mindful of what I consume. I try to limit my time on social media to a few times a week, which helps me avoid getting caught up in the endless stream of misinformation. When I do come across fake content, I take a step back and try to verify the information before sharing it or engaging with it. It's not always easy, but I've found that being more cautious and critical helps me navigate the online world with greater ease. I also try to surround myself with people who share my values and are more likely to fact-check and critically think about the information they share. It's not about completely avoiding social media, but about being more intentional and discerning in how I use it. I've also taken up volunteering as a hobby, which has given me a new perspective on what's truly important and helped me stay grounded in the face of online noise. All in all, it's a constant work in progress, but being more aware and intentional has helped me manage the stress and anxiety that comes with dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: I've come to realize that dealing with fake content is a constant battle, so I set boundaries and am more mindful of what I consume on social media. I limit my time on social media to a few times a week and verify information before sharing it or engaging with it. I also try to surround myself with people who share my values and are fact-checkers. Volunteering has given me a new perspective on what's truly important and helped me stay grounded in the face of online noise. It's a constant work in progress, but being more aware and intentional has helped me manage the stress and anxiety that comes with dealing with fake content on social media.
